data.title: Nvidia Stock Forecast Ahead of Earnings – Here's What to Expect
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA) is set to release its fourth-quarter fiscal 2026 results on February 25. With AI GPU demand staying robust, investors will look for confirmation that revenue and margins can support current valuations. The chip giant's results mark a pivotal moment for investors tracking its lead amid booming GPU demand and competitive pressures. With shares trading around $185–$190 recently, Wall Street anticipates robust results that could propel the stock higher or expose valuation risks.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Nvidia dominates the AI infrastructure market with an estimated 80% GPU share through its H100/H200 AI chips and emerging Blackwell platforms. At the same time, Nvidia faces an ongoing battle over China market recovery, Rubin GPU timelines, and hyperscaler spending. Over the past year, NVDA shares have surged nearly 45%.

Expectations from Nvidia

According to Wall Street estimates, Nvidia is expected to post adjusted earnings per share (EPS) of $1.52, up nearly 71% from the year-ago figure of $0.89. Moreover, sales are forecast to jump 67% year-over-year to $65.56 billion.

For full-year fiscal 2026, sales are projected at $213.3 billion and adjusted earnings at $4.69 per share. Data center sales have been a major revenue driver for Nvidia through the first nine months of the fiscal year, totaling $131.4 billion.

Analysts' Views Ahead of Results

Heading into the results, a majority of analysts have reiterated their Buy ratings on NVDA stock. UBS analyst Timothy Arcuri lifted his price target from $235 to $245, implying 28.9% upside. He noted that the supply chain remains strong and the setup into the next earnings period is favorable, particularly with the GTC developer conference coming up in mid-March.

Arcuri also believes Nvidia will exclude China sales from its guidance since market reopening is still uncertain amid rising domestic chip adoption. Profitability questions persist as peers build AI processors. UBS sees little near-term risk to Nvidia's roughly 75% gross margin and expects management to address competitive concerns during the earnings call and GTC.

Similarly, Goldman Sachs analyst James Schneider maintained a $250 price target ahead of the results, suggesting 31.5% upside. He expects Nvidia's Q4 sales to hit $67.3 billion, implying a $2 billion revenue surprise above the consensus, and a bottom-line beat.

Schneider highlighted drivers that could push Nvidia's stock higher after the results, including stronger-than-expected hyperscalers' capex, positive comments on FY27 data-center sales forecast, a ramp in GPU demand from LLM companies, the competitive advantages of Nvidia's CUDA platform, China demand trends, and progress on Rubin production.

Is Nvidia a Good Stock to Buy?

On TipRanks, Nvidia stock commands a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 37 Buys, one Hold, and one Sell rating. The average Nvidia price target of $260.38 implies 37% upside potential from current levels.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: AppLovin Stock (APP) Falls Despite Beating Q4 Expectations, Wiliam Blair Stays Bullish
data.description:
AppLovin (APP) stock fell about 4% in Thursday's pre-market trading despite the ad-tech platform reporting better-than-expected results for the fourth quarter of 2025. The company also issued upbeat guidance for the first quarter of 2026. Heading into Q4 earnings, some analysts were concerned about APP stock's elevated valuation and growing competition in the digital ads market.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

As of Wednesday's closing, APP stock was down 32.2% year-to-date, though it has risen about 22% over the past year.

Key Insights from AppLovin's Q4 Earnings

AppLovin reported a 66% growth in its Q4 2025 revenue to $1.66 billion, exceeding analysts' consensus estimate of $1.61 billion. The company attributed the top-line strength to continued technology advancements in its core mobile gaming business, seasonal strength, and the favorable impact of its e-commerce initiative.

Meanwhile, APP's adjusted earnings per share (EPS) jumped more than 87% year-over-year to $3.24 and crushed Wall Street's estimate of $2.95. The robust bottom line reflected strong revenue growth and solid margin expansion.

Looking ahead, AppLovin expects Q1 2026 revenue in the range of $1.745 billion to $1.775 billion and adjusted EBITDA (earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization) in the range of $1.465 billion to $1.495 billion. The company also expects continued strength in its margins. While the Q1 2026 revenue guidance exceeded expectations, it indicates a slowdown (52% growth at midpoint) compared to the fourth quarter of 2025.

When questioned about growing competition in the digital ads space, CEO Adam Foroughi expressed confidence in AppLovin's expertise in the niche market.

William Blair Analyst Reacts Positively

Following the Q4 print, William Blair analyst Ralph Schackart reiterated a Buy rating on APP stock, saying that the company delivered the "strongest operating performance to date" despite AI (artificial intelligence) concerns.

He noted that management addressed bears' concerns during the earnings call to help mitigate the disconnect between current market sentiment and the company's operating performance. Notably, management highlighted that if AI was disrupting the business, it would be tough for the company to grow its revenue by 66% and achieve 84% adjusted EBITDA margin.

Addressing concerns about growing competition, Foroughi explained that AI tools like Google's (GOOGL) Project Genie can significantly reduce content-creation costs and lead to a surge in new games. That said, as more games enter the market, finding the best ones could become tough — and that's where advertisers will turn to AppLovin's platform.

Is APP a Good Stock to Buy?

Currently, Wall Street has a Strong Buy consensus rating on AppLovin stock based on 15 unanimous Buys. The average APP stock price target of $745.15 indicates 63.1% upside potential from current levels.

These ratings/price targets could change as more analysts react to AppLovin's Q4 results and guidance.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Tesla Stock (TSLA) Rises as Chinese Watchdog Moves to Tame Cutthroat Price War
data.description:
American electric vehicle maker Tesla's (TSLA) shares rose fractionally on Thursday. The move came as Chinese authorities released new price-compliance guidance for automakers, including a ban on selling vehicles below production cost.

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

The new guide from the State Administration for Market Regulation (SAMR) comes as Tesla in January offered zero-interest loans of up to five years to customers in China, igniting a new bout of competition over loan terms among automakers in the world's largest car market.

The guide also follows reports of weaker consumer demand for vehicles in January. The new rules apply to both carmakers, dealers, and parts suppliers.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: AeroVironment Stock (AVAV) Gains on Real-World Anti-Drone Laser Deployment
data.description:
AeroVironment (AVAV), a defense company, saw its technology used in a real-world deployment this week. The U.S. Army used its LOCUST counter-drone laser near El Paso International Airport on Wednesday, according to a Reuters report. The system was placed at Fort Bliss, right next to the airport.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The activity led regulators to pause flights for about seven hours as a safety precaution. Flights later resumed, and authorities said there was no threat to commercial aircraft.

What the LOCUST System Does

LOCUST is built to stop small drones. Instead of firing a missile, it focuses a beam of energy onto a flying object and disables it within seconds. A laser system mainly uses electricity, which makes each shot far less expensive than using a missile.

The Army received its first LOCUST units in 2024 and tested them in Arizona before training soldiers in Oklahoma. The El Paso use appears to be one of the clearest examples of the system operating near a civilian area.

U.S. officials said drone sightings along the southern border now exceed 1,000 a month. Some are used for surveillance and smuggling and can also pose risks near airports and large events.

With major events planned this year, including the FIFA World Cup, officials are seeking safer ways to stop drones. Counter-drone lasers like LOCUST could become part of that effort, especially around cities and sensitive sites.

Is AeroVironment Stock a Good Buy Right Now?   

Analysts remain bullish about AeroVironment's stock trajectory. With 13 unanimous Buy ratings, AVAV stock commands a Strong Buy consensus rating on TipRanks. Also, the average AVAV price target of $390.75 implies about 60.53% upside potential from current levels.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Applied Materials (AMAT) Clears China Export Hurdle Ahead of Q1 Earnings
data.description:
Applied Materials (AMAT) has reached an agreement with the U.S. Department of Commerce over alleged export control issues involving China. The resolution removes a key regulatory hurdle just as the company prepares to report its Q1 earnings, giving investors clarity ahead of the quarterly results.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

For context, Applied Materials designs and manufactures equipment used to produce microchips, including those for AI applications.

What to Expect from Applied Materials' Q1 Earnings?

Analysts expect Applied Materials to report earnings per share (EPS) of $2.21 for Q1 FY26, compared to the $2.38 per share posted in the same period last year.

Meanwhile, management expects first-quarter revenue of roughly $6.85 billion, slightly down from $7.17 billion a year ago. Overall, demand for the company's advanced tools is being fueled by strong capital spending from AI-focused data centers. However, a slowdown in wafer fab equipment spending in China could pressure results, particularly as trade restrictions have narrowed Applied Materials' addressable market.

Applied Materials Resolves Issue with Commerce Department

As part of the agreement, Applied Materials will pay $252.5 million to resolve civil allegations from the Bureau of Industry and Security (BIS) concerning certain shipments to China between November 2020 and July 2022.

The company said the U.S. Department of Justice and the Securities and Exchange Commission have also closed their related investigations without taking further action.

AMAT's Key Updates Ahead of Earnings

Ahead of earnings, Applied Materials announced that Samsung Electronics (SSNLF) will join its new $5 billion EPIC Center in Silicon Valley. Opening this year, the EPIC Center will be the world's largest and most advanced facility for developing semiconductor manufacturing technology and equipment. The company's CEO, Gary Dickerson, said the growing global demand for energy-efficient AI chips is pushing the semiconductor industry to innovate faster. With Samsung at the EPIC Center, both teams will collaborate closely to speed up the development and launch of advanced manufacturing technologies.

Additionally, the company has recently rolled out new chipmaking systems for deposition, etching, and changing materials. These tools make the tiniest parts of advanced chips more efficient, which helps AI computers run faster and use less energy.

Is Applied Materials a Good Stock to Buy?

According to TipRanks, AMAT stock has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 22 Buys and two Holds assigned in the last three months. At $360.19, the Applied Materials' average stock price target implies a 6% upside potential.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Why Cathie Wood Dumped Millions in Airbnb and Teradyne
data.description:
Cathie Wood's ARK Invest ETFs (exchange-traded funds) reveal their daily fund disclosures, offering insights into the fund's buy and sell trades. On Wednesday, February 11, Wood offloaded a major chunk of Airbnb (ABNB) and trimmed exposure to Teradyne (TER), reaping millions that were strategically reinvested in other promising companies.

The Airbnb Trade

Wood sold 186,757 shares of the vacation rental company for $22.33 million, just ahead of its Q4FY25 results due today. The Street expects Airbnb to post adjusted earnings of $0.67 per share, reflecting an 8.2% year-over-year decline. Revenue is forecasted to reach $2.71 billion, up about 9.3% from the same quarter last year.

Investors are watching the rollout of experiences and services, the shift toward faster-growth markets outside the core, how management will handle weaker demand in core markets, and the short-term impact on margins from recent growth investments.

Is ABNB Stock a Buy?

data.title: Cathie Wood Hunts for Value as ARK Invest Snaps Up Robinhood During the $66k Bitcoin Slump
data.description:
Cathie Wood's ARK Invest is leaning into the volatility today, as the firm aggressively bought the dip in crypto-adjacent stocks. While Bitcoin briefly slipped below the $66,000 mark and sparked an ETF exodus, ARK moved in the opposite direction. The Robinhood (HOOD) purchase alone totaled roughly $33.8 million. This highlights Wood's belief that the current price weakness is a temporary hurdle rather than a long-term trend.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

HOOD Conducted a Testnet Launch of the Robinhood Chain

The timing of ARK's acquisition is particularly notable because it follows the official testnet launch of the Robinhood Chain. This new permissionless Layer 2 (L2) blockchain is designed specifically for tokenized real-world assets and institutional financial services. Although Robinhood's stock price slid nearly 9% following a slight revenue miss in its Q4 report, ARK appears to be looking past the quarterly earnings noise to focus on the company's evolution into a core blockchain infrastructure provider.

ARK Focuses on Fintech

This latest round of bottom-fishing has officially made Robinhood the largest crypto-linked position in the flagship ARK Innovation ETF (ARKK). The fund now holds approximately $248 million in HOOD shares, representing a 4.1% weighting. Alongside the Robinhood buy, ARK also expanded its exposure to the crypto exchange Bullish (BLSH) and the USDC issuer Circle (CRCL), adding another $16 million in combined shares as those assets traded lower during the Wednesday sell-off.

Broader Market Remains Chaotic

The broader crypto market remains in a state of flux since U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded $276.3 million in net outflows on Wednesday. This move nearly wiped out the weekly gains for these funds and brought total assets under management to $85.7 billion, their lowest level since late 2024. While Bitcoin has since stabilized around $67,200, the lack of immediate momentum in the ETF space suggests that many institutional buyers are waiting for a clearer signal before jumping back in.

Key Takeaway

In short, Cathie Wood is doing exactly what she always does, which is buying the stocks that everyone else is running away from. While the rest of the market is panicking about Bitcoin dropping under $66,000 and Robinhood missing its revenue targets by a hair, ARK is betting that the Robinhood Chain will positively impact how stocks are traded.

Is Robinhood a Good Stock to Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on HOOD stock based on 14 Buys, three Holds, and zero Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average 12-month HOOD price target of $135.79 per share implies 56.9% upside potential.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Strategy Stock Weathers the Storm as Michael Saylor Says, 'We'll Buy Bitcoin Every Quarter Forever'
data.description:
Strategy (MSTR) is demonstrating a buy-at-any-cost mentality today, since the stock is trading around $130.31 despite a brutal week for digital assets. While many firms would scale back during a market slide, Executive Chairman Michael Saylor is signaling the opposite. Strategy stock is currently acting as the primary vehicle for his plan to use creative Wall Street instruments to vacuum up a historic percentage of the total Bitcoin (BTC-USD) supply.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Saylor Creates an Accumulation Engine

Saylor appeared on CNBC's Squawk Box this week to reassure investors that the company has no plans to sell its 714,644 BTC stash. Instead, the firm is leaning into its AI-designed preferred stock programs, such as STRK and STRF, to raise new capital. These securities offer fixed dividends reaching as high as 11.25%; consequently, they allow Strategy to raise billions from income-focused investors while using every cent of the proceeds to buy more Bitcoin.

Preferred Stock Provides a Volatility Buffer

The move toward issuing preferred shares instead of just common stock allows Saylor to raise money without diluting existing shareholders as aggressively during price dips. TD Cowen analysts noted that these "STR" shares offer a way for retirees and conservative funds to gain Bitcoin exposure with a steady yield. Furthermore, they provide the firm with a massive cash cushion to survive a prolonged crypto bear market.

Market Eyes the $406 Price as a Recovery Target

Investor sentiment remains split as short interest in the stock has climbed 40% since late last year. Bears argue that the company is over-leveraged, but CEO Phong Le recently countered that the balance sheet is built to withstand a 90% crash in Bitcoin prices. If the market finds a floor here, many Wall Street analysts expect a violent melt-up toward a consensus price target of $406, driven by the stock's tendency to trade at a significant premium to its actual Bitcoin holdings.

Is Strategy Inc a Buy or Sell?

According to TipRanks, MSTR stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 11 Wall Street analysts. This rating is based on a unanimous 11 Buy ratings assigned in the past three months. The average 12-month MSTR price target of $359.82 implies 185.4% upside from current levels.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Why Are Stock Market Futures Up Today, 2/12/26?
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were higher ahead of Thursday's session after the Dow's three-day winning streak ended and investors waited for fresh economic data. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), and the S&P 500 (SPX) Index were up 0.07%, 0.21%, and 0.22%, respectively, at 1:48 a.m. EDT on February 12.

Valentine's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In after-hours moves, Cisco Systems (CSCO) fell about 7% after weak quarterly guidance, while McDonald's (MCD) slipped slightly despite beating earnings estimates.

The declines followed a soft session on Wall Street, with the Dow down about 66 points, or 0.1%, while the S&P 500 slipped roughly 0.1% and the Nasdaq fell about 0.2%.

Earlier, stocks had risen on a strong jobs report but later lost momentum. January payrolls increased by 130,000, and unemployment dipped to 4.3%, easing worries about the labor market.

On Thursday, traders will also look at weekly jobless claims and the existing home sales report for more economic signals.
